name: Apple Music (MusicKit)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Apple Music (MusicKit)

MUST DO (non-negotiable):
- Request authorization as soon as the screen appears: call MusicAuthorization.request() in .onAppear (or in a task when the main view loads). Do not wait for the user to tap a search/play button — authorization must be requested up front.
- The "Build Playlist", "Create and Play", or any search/play button MUST be .disabled until MusicAuthorization.request() has returned .authorized. Use a property like isAuthorized, set it to true only when status == .authorized, and bind the button: .disabled(!viewModel.isAuthorized). If not authorized, show text like "Authorize Apple Music first" and a button that calls requestAuthorization().
- There must be ZERO code that requests, mentions, or uses a "developer token". On iOS the phrase "developer token" is wrong — delete any such code. Use ONLY MusicAuthorization.request(); the system handles tokens after that.
- When search or playback fails, show a user-friendly message (e.g. "Could not access Apple Music" or "Playback failed"). If the error suggests access was denied, also show a hint: "Check Settings > Music and ensure you're signed in with an Apple Music subscription."

MusicKit lets you search the Apple Music catalog and play songs with ApplicationMusicPlayer. The user must have an Apple Music subscription for full catalog access.

IMPORTS:
- `import MusicKit` for catalog search and playback.

AUTHORIZATION (required before any MusicKit API):
1. Request once: `let status = await MusicAuthorization.request()`
2. Check: `switch status { case .authorized: ... case .denied: ... case .notDetermined: ... }`
3. Show clear UI if denied (e.g. "Allow Music access in Settings").
4. Info.plist key NSAppleMusicUsageDescription is added automatically by the build system.
5. Do NOT enable "Create and Play" or search/playback until status == .authorized. Disable the button or show "Authorize Apple Music first" until then.

CRITICAL — NO DEVELOPER TOKEN ON IOS:
- On native iOS, do NOT use or request a "developer token". MusicKit handles tokens automatically after MusicAuthorization.request(). Any code that requests, generates, or references a developer token is wrong for iOS and causes "failed to request developer token" and crashes.
- Use only MusicAuthorization.request() for user-based access; no backend or JWT required.

CATALOG SEARCH:
- Use `MusicCatalogSearchRequest(term: "Artist Name", types: [Song.self])` or include [Artist.self], [Album.self] as needed.
- Execute: `let response = try await request.response()`
- Songs: `response.songs` (MusicItemCollection<Song>). Each Song has .title, .artistName, .duration, .artwork, etc.
- Song duration is a TimeInterval (seconds). Use it to build playlists by total duration.

PLAYBACK:
- Use `ApplicationMusicPlayer.shared` (applicationMusicPlayer).
- Set queue: `applicationMusicPlayer.queue = ApplicationMusicPlayer.Queue(for: songCollection)` or assign an array of Song. Set the full queue before calling play; avoid building the queue incrementally.
- Start: `try await applicationMusicPlayer.play()`
- Control: .pause(), .stop(), .skipToNextEntry(), .skipToPreviousEntry()
- State: applicationMusicPlayer.state (playbackState, currentEntry, etc.)

BUILDING A PLAYLIST BY DURATION (e.g. "play me songs by Drake for 30 mins"):
1. Request MusicAuthorization if not determined.
2. Search: MusicCatalogSearchRequest(term: artistOrQuery, types: [Song.self]); get response.songs.
3. Pick songs (e.g. from response.songs) and sum durations until totalDuration >= requestedMinutes * 60. Assume ~3–4 min per song if duration not available; otherwise use song.duration.
4. Build an array of Song from the selected items, then set queue: applicationMusicPlayer.queue = ApplicationMusicPlayer.Queue(for: selectedSongs).
5. try await applicationMusicPlayer.play().

VOICE → PLAYLIST FLOW:
- Use Speech framework (SFSpeechRecognizer) to get the user's phrase (e.g. "play me songs by Drake for 30 minutes").
- Parse the phrase for: artist/query and duration (e.g. 30 mins). Simple parsing: look for numbers + "min" or "minutes", and the rest as artist/search term.
- Then run the MusicKit flow above: authorize → search → build queue by duration → play.

DATA MODEL:
- Song and other MusicKit types are already Identifiable where appropriate. For queue entries, use MusicPlayer.Queue.Entry; it holds the playable item.

ERROR HANDLING (mandatory):
- Never crash on MusicKit or search/playback errors. Wrap all MusicKit calls (authorization, search, play) in do/catch or handle errors in async code.
- Show errors in UI: set an errorMessage string and display it (e.g. "Could not search: …" or "Playback failed: …"). Do not force unwrap, fatalError, or throw on API failures.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT use or request a "developer token" on iOS — MusicKit handles tokens automatically after MusicAuthorization.request().
- Do NOT call MusicKit APIs before requesting and checking MusicAuthorization — playback will fail.
- Do NOT enable Create and Play before authorization is .authorized — disable the button until then.
- Do NOT force unwrap in view bodies (e.g. Text(song!.title)) — use optional binding or nil-coalescing.
- Do NOT assume response.songs is non-empty — handle empty results ("No songs found").
- Do NOT let API errors crash the app — always catch and show in UI.
- For duration-based playlists, do NOT assume every Song has a valid duration — use a default (e.g. 240 seconds) when missing.
antiPatterns: Using or requesting a developer token on iOS — use only MusicAuthorization.request()
Enabling Create and Play before authorization is .authorized
Force unwrapping MusicKit results in view bodies
Assuming response.songs is non-empty without checking
Letting MusicKit/search/playback errors crash the app — always catch and show in UI
Ignoring missing Song.duration when building duration-based playlists

commonErrors: Failed to request developer token (remove all developer token code; use only MusicAuthorization.request())
Create and Play enabled before authorization (must .disabled(!isAuthorized) until .authorized)

Search failed / Could not access Apple Music (request MusicAuthorization.request() in .onAppear; disable Build Playlist until isAuthorized; user may need to authorize in Settings or have Apple Music subscription)
